content.full_text:
RAvI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
October 23,
1996
Nancy Stuart Grisham
Senior Deputy County Counsel
County of Placer
175 Fuiweiler Avenue
Auburn, California
95603
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—96-272
Dear Ms.
Grisham:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
The Placer County Mosquito Abatement District has proposed an
informational flier
(copy attached)
regarding an upcoming vote on
Measure M,
a proposed special tax to fund mosquito control.
The
district would like to have this flier available for people who
ask for information on the tax.
The flier will not be mailed to
the public,
but will be available for people to pick up at the
office or at informational meetings.
From the information you have submitted,
a reportable
expenditure would not result.
(Section 82025 and Regulation
18225,
copy enclosed.)
Under Regulation 18225(a) (1),
a payment is
“made for political purposes” if it is:
“For the purpose of influencing or attempting to
influence the action of the voters for or against the
passage of any measure
.. . .“
Under Regulation 18225(b),
an “expenditure” includes any
monetary or non—monetary payment made by any person that is used
for communications which expressly advocate the passage or defeat
of a clearly identified ballot measure.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
Commission regulations
appear at Title 2,
Sections 18000-18995 of the California Code of
Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—96—272
Page
2
A communication “expressly advocates” the passage or defeat
of a measure if it refers to a clearly identified measure so that
the communication, taken as a whole,
unambiguously urges a
particular result in an election.
(Regulation 18225(b) (2)..)
Since the district flier does not expressly advocate the
passage or defeat of Measure M, the above expenditure provisions
do not apply.
The flier would be considered a contribution if it were
printed and mailed “at the behest of” a candidate or ballot
measure committee.
(Regulation 18215(a)
and (b), Regulation
18225.7,
copies enclosed.)
On October 13,
1996,
you stated that
the informational flier was proposed by the Mosquito Abatement
Board, solely to explain the measure to the public.
Therefore,
pursuant to your facts and the regulations cited above,
a
contribution has not been made to any ballot measure committee.
I trust this answers your question.
Sincerely,
Steven C.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Co
sel,
Legal Division
SGC:JS:ak
Enclosures

sections.conclusion: Since the district flier does not expressly advocate the passage or defeat of Measure M, the expenditure provisions do not apply. Pursuant to the facts and the regulations cited above, a contribution has not been made to any ballot measure committee.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an informational flier regarding Measure M (a proposed special tax for mosquito control) distributed by the Placer County Mosquito Abatement District constitutes a reportable expenditure or contribution under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The informational flier does not constitute a reportable expenditure or contribution because it does not expressly advocate for passage or defeat of the measure and was proposed solely by the District Board to explain the measure, not at the behest of a candidate or ballot measure committee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present in document. Question and conclusion synthesized from letter content. Document contains substantive regulatory analysis with citations to Government Code sections and FPPC regulations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether an informational flier regarding Measure M (a proposed special tax for mosquito control) distributed by the Placer County Mosquito Abatement District constitutes a reportable expenditure or contribution under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Since the district flier does not expressly advocate the passage or defeat of Measure M, the expenditure provisions do not apply. Pursuant to the facts and the regulations cited above, a contribution has not been made to any ballot measure committee.

The district would like to have this flier available for people who ask for information on the tax. The flier will not be mailed to the public, but will be available for people to pick up at the office or at informational meetings. From the information you have submitted, a reportable expenditure would not result. (Section 82025 and Regulation 18225, copy enclosed.) Under Regulation 18225(a) (1), a payment is “made for political purposes” if it is: “For the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against the passage of any measure .. . .“ Under Regulation 18225(b), an “expenditure” includes any monetary or non—monetary payment made by any person that is used for communications which expressly advocate the passage or defeat of a clearly identified ballot measure. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, Sections 18000-18995 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660 File No. A—96—272 Page 2 A communication “expressly advocates” the passage or defeat of a measure if it refers to a clearly identified measure so that the communication, taken as a whole, unambiguously urges a particular result in an election. (Regulation 18225(b) (2)..) Since the district flier does not expressly advocate the passage or defeat of Measure M, the above expenditure provisions do not apply. The flier would be considered a contribution if it were printed and mailed “at the behest of” a candidate or ballot measure committee. (Regulation 18215(a) and (b), Regulation 18225.7, copies enclosed.) On October 13, 1996, you stated that the informational flier was proposed by the Mosquito Abatement Board, solely to explain the measure to the public. Therefore, pursuant to your facts and the regulations cited above, a contribution has not been made to any ballot measure committee. I trust this answers your question. Sincerely, Steven C. Churchwell General Counsel By: Co sel, Legal Division SGC:JS:ak Enclosures
embedding.summary: The FPPC advised that an informational flier regarding Measure M distributed by the Placer County Mosquito Abatement District does not constitute a reportable expenditure or contribution under the Political Reform Act because it lacks express advocacy for or against the measure.
